title: aquilegia coerulea var. alpina
intro: Aquilegia coerulea var. alpina is a type of flowering plant, also known as the alpine columbine, which is a subspecies of the coerulea species and is native to the mountainous regions of Western North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and reducing pain. The health benefits of Aquilegia coerulea var. alpina include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and help with respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
